scenarios.title: Accidental liquid damage repair within plan term
scenarios.description: The customer reports that their iPhone 13 Pro was accidentally exposed to water causing it to malfunction. The incident occurred within 24 months of plan purchase and the device has not previously used any ADH claims.

service_fee.notes: Service fee applies per section 3.2 for each ADH claim

policy_references.snippet: Apple covers operational failure from accidental damage such as liquid contact, subject to a $49 service fee per claim and maximum 2 Service Events during the Plan Term.
Coverage is valid within 24 months of the original retail purchase date of the iPhone.

scenarios.reasoning: The accidental liquid damage occurred within the 24-month Plan Term and is an unexpected external event covered under section 3.2. The claim is valid as this is the first ADH claim and a $49 service fee applies. The device qualifies for repair or replacement under the accidental damage coverage.

scenarios.title: Speaker malfunction without accidental damage within plan term
scenarios.description: Customer’s iPhone 13 speaker stopped producing sound after 10 months of use with no accidental damage involved.

service_fee.notes: No service fee for hardware defects under section 3.1

policy_references.snippet: If during the Plan Term, a defect in materials and workmanship arises, Apple will repair or exchange the Covered iPhone at no charge.
Coverage is valid for 24 months from the original retail purchase date of the iPhone.

scenarios.reasoning: The failure is due to a hardware defect occurring within the 24-month plan term with no accidental damage involved, making it eligible for repair or replacement without a service fee as per section 3.1.

scenarios.title: Front camera fails due to manufacturing defect within plan term
scenarios.description: Customer reports that the iPhone 13 front camera stopped functioning properly 14 months after purchase, with no accidental damage or external causes.

service_fee.notes: No service fee applies for defects in materials and workmanship

policy_references.snippet: If during the Plan Term, a defect in materials and workmanship has arisen in the Covered iPhone, Apple will repair or exchange the device at no charge.
Coverage ends twenty-four (24) months from the date of retail purchase of your original iPhone (Plan Term).

scenarios.reasoning: The front camera failure is a defect arising within the 24-month plan term and not caused by accidental damage, triggering coverage under section 3.1. Since this is a hardware defect claim, no service fee applies as per policy terms.

scenarios.title: Touchscreen stops responding due to hardware defect within plan term
scenarios.description: The iPhone touchscreen has become unresponsive with no evidence of accidental damage, reported 20 months after purchase.

service_fee.notes: No service fee applies for non-ADH hardware defects

policy_references.snippet: If during the Plan Term, a defect in materials and workmanship has arisen in the Covered iPhone, Apple will repair or exchange the device at no charge.
Coverage ends twenty-four (24) months from the date of retail purchase of the original iPhone.

scenarios.reasoning: The touchscreen failure is a defect in materials or workmanship arising during the 24-month Plan Term and is not caused by accidental damage, so it is fully covered with no service fee as per section 3.1 and 2.
